title: Harden Argus wire transport, protocol references, and manual coverage
operator_request: Argus must be the named native eyes for every model, headless and non-intrusive, documented in the internal manual and usable by parallel agents.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Resume by inspecting MT-007 diff first. Add only the missing Argus wire/protocol/manual hardening; do not relaunch Handshake in foreground.

scope.summary: Prove Argus aliases over the real MCP wire path, document the methods in the internal UserManual, and keep the Argus protocol as the visual proof requirement for GUI work.
base_scope: Add TCP/named-pipe-level tests for argus.inspect, argus.click, argus.set_value, argus.screenshot, and unauthorized argus.* calls.
Document argus.inspect, argus.click, argus.set_value, and argus.screenshot in the internal UserManual.
Document the inspect -> select stable author_id -> steer -> re-inspect -> screenshot/snapshot evidence loop.
Ensure Argus protocol references state that inability to see or steer a GUI surface is technical debt and allowed same-WP remediation scope.
Keep Argus quiet: no foreground pop, focus steal, keyboard hijack, or OS mouse movement.
acceptance_criteria: Wire-level Argus calls return the same result shapes as the native MCP primitives plus Argus metadata.
Unauthorized Argus calls fail before reading snapshots, taking leases, logging actions, or enqueueing actions.
Manual coverage names Argus methods and non-intrusive constraints.
Protocol coverage requires Argus visual inspection for GUI claims.
verification: Focused cargo tests for Argus unit and wire paths.
Manual-content tests for Argus topic strings.
Code search proving no foreground/input-stealing APIs were introduced.

handoff.summary: Implemented: Argus wire/protocol/manual hardening. TCP and Windows named-pipe Argus coverage pass; unauthorized wire auth now returns before snapshot lock; protocol/startup/HBR references name argus.* as canonical. Awaiting validator.

commands_run.evidence: 1 passed, 0 failed; wrong-token argus.inspect returns before waiting for snapshot lock.
1 passed, 0 failed; bad token wire shape remains -32001 Unauthorized.
1 passed, 0 failed; oversized buffered newline rejected before dispatch.
1 passed, 0 failed on Windows; named pipe drives argus.inspect, unauthorized argus.click, argus.click, argus.set_value, and argus.screenshot.
5 passed, 0 failed; serial TCP Argus wire proof.
7 passed, 0 failed; Argus facade/manual lib coverage.
1 passed, 0 failed; structured manual rows use real tools.

commands_run.command: rg -n "Until a dedicated|dedicated Rust-native Argus command exists|Argus-compatible path" .GOV/roles_shared/docs/ARGUS_VISUAL_INSPECTION_PROTOCOL.md .GOV/roles/coder/docs/CODER_STARTUP_BRIEF.md .GOV/roles_shared/docs/SHARED_STARTUP_BRIEF.md .GOV/roles_shared/records/HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json
commands_run.evidence: PASS: no stale future/placeholder Argus wording in touched authority surfaces.
PASS_WITH_COMMENT_MATCHES: matches are comments documenting forbidden APIs, no call sites.

results.summary: MT-008 post-review PASS after remediation: unauthorized wire requests return before snapshot lock; named-pipe Argus methods are covered; protocol/startup/HBR references use canonical argus.* methods.

adversarial_review.summary: Initial FAIL on auth-before-snapshot and named-pipe Argus coverage; both remediated and re-reviewed PASS.
